source.title: Surficial Geologic Map of the New York City Area (fixture)
source.license: Public domain (synthetic data crafted for the geo-nyc demo)

borehole_control_points.label: BH-1 Battery Park
borehole_control_points.note: Shallow bedrock near Battery Park — schist outcrops close to surface.

borehole_control_points.label: BH-2 City Hall
borehole_control_points.note: Moderate depth — glacial deposits thicken moving north from the Battery.

borehole_control_points.label: BH-3 Midtown West
borehole_control_points.note: Deep buried valley axis — bedrock drops sharply beneath thick glacial fill.

borehole_control_points.label: BH-4 East Midtown
borehole_control_points.note: Bedrock rises toward the east side — Manhattan Schist ridge.

borehole_control_points.label: BH-5 Upper West Side
borehole_control_points.note: Moderate depth — glacial deposits thin toward Inwood.

borehole_control_points.label: BH-6 Central Park North
borehole_control_points.note: Schist outcrops in Central Park — very shallow bedrock.

borehole_control_points.label: BH-7 Harlem
borehole_control_points.note: Shallow bedrock — Inwood Marble / Schist contact zone.

borehole_control_points.label: BH-8 West Village
borehole_control_points.note: Moderate depth — transition zone between Battery shallow and Midtown deep.

borehole_control_points.label: BH-9 Lower East Side
borehole_control_points.note: Moderate depth — East River proximity, thicker alluvial deposits.

notes: Hand-crafted fixture loosely modelled on the Lower Manhattan stratigraphy with spatially varying borehole control points reflecting real NYC bedrock topography (shallow at Battery/Harlem, deep buried valley in Midtown). Used by /api/run when GEO_NYC_USE_FIXTURES=true.
